one_line_takeaway: Doc-PP shows that LVLMs answering ordinary document-QA queries under explicit non-disclosure policies leak protected values far more often when the answer requires cross-modal/multi-page reasoning (up to 93.5% leakage) than when directly asked, and that a lightweight Decompose-Verify-Aggregate inference-time framework roughly halves leakage versus CoT/post-hoc revision baselines, though at a faithfulness and verbosity cost and without fully closing indirect/contextual-inference leaks.
key_finding: Under implicit (reasoning-heavy) queries with policy applied, leakage is dramatically higher than under explicit queries across every tested model, e.g. image-input leakage: GPT-5.2 8.1 (explicit) vs 25.7 (implicit); Gemini-3-Pro-Preview 31.4 vs 46.8; Gemini-3-Flash-Preview 49.4 vs 64.6; Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct 75.6 vs 93.5; Mistral-Large-2512 45.8 vs 76.8. OCR-based text input raises leakage relative to image input for the three models tested in both conditions (e.g., GPT-5.2 explicit-query leakage 8.1->11.5; Gemini-3-Pro-Preview 31.4->28.7 [one exception]; Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking 11.5->14.2). Evidence-type breakdown for Gemini-3-Flash-Preview (Table 4) shows leakage rising sharply with cross-modal complexity: implicit-query leakage is 72.8 for single text, 75.2 for single table, 67.9 for chart, but reaches 100.0 for 'Table (Multi)' and 100.0 for 'Chart + Text' combined evidence. Document-type analysis (Gemini-3-Flash-Preview) shows business reports (68.1% text ratio) have the highest leakage at 84.6%, versus research reports (50.3% text ratio) at 62.2% -- the authors interpret higher textual density as 'guiding' the model toward computing protected values from visuals. Context-window size (±2 to ±25 pages) does not systematically reduce leakage (fluctuates 60-70% throughout), indicating leakage is driven by localized cross-modal reasoning rather than long-range context dilution. For mitigation (Table 5, default vs DVA leakage): Gemini-3-Flash-Preview 64.6->30.5; Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct 93.5->24.5; Mistral-Large-2512 76.8->41.6. CoT and Revision give smaller reductions (e.g., Gemini-3-Flash-Preview: CoT 51.0, Revision 38.8, still above DVA's 30.5). A qualitative case study (37% approval-rating example) shows CoT and Revision still leak the protected 37% figure while DVA's element-level verification discards the invalid element and answers without it. DVA does have a faithfulness/verbosity cost noted in the appendix: average response length grows (Naive 382 tokens -> CoT 552 -> Revision 862 -> DVA 936 tokens) and faithfulness for DVA in the aggregated 3-model comparison (Table 9) is 55.8, lower than Revision's 66.9 and CoT's 59.0, despite DVA's leakage being lowest (30.5 vs 38.8 and 51.0).
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is twofold: (1) Doc-PP, a benchmark of 443 explicit and 698 implicit queries (1141 total confidential targets) built from 90 real-world long-form PDF reports (financial, business, research/introduction, administration/industry files), each paired with a natural-language non-disclosure policy ('Do not disclose {target}') and evaluated for Leakage (does the answer contain the confidential value v) and Faithfulness (does the answer cover a filtered content-coverage checklist without disclosing the protected value); and (2) DVA (Decompose-Verify-Aggregation), a two-stage inference-time mitigation (Decompose the draft answer into atomic elements; Verify each element against the policy and Aggregate only the valid ones into the final answer) that the authors propose and evaluate as a defense, contrasted against Chain-of-Thought prompting and post-hoc revision baselines.
threat_model: The threat is not an adversarial attacker crafting jailbreak prompts; rather it is inadvertent policy violation by a benign LVLM operating under a legitimate, user-defined non-disclosure constraint (e.g., 'Do not disclose revenue information for the Middle East region') while answering ordinary document QA queries. The 'attack surface' the paper documents is the model's own cross-modal reasoning: sensitive values are never stated directly but must be inferred/computed by combining a chart value with a text total, or by aggregating evidence across multiple pages/modalities, which lets the model bypass its own compliance behavior. Two specific failure modes are named by the authors as their own findings: the 'Reasoning-Induced Safety Gap' (leakage rates spike when queries are implicit/reasoning-heavy versus explicit) and the 'OCR Paradox' (providing Mistral-OCR-extracted text alongside images improves perceptual clarity but increases leakage relative to image-only input, e.g., GPT-5.2 leakage under implicit+policy rises from 25.7 with image input to 25.8 with OCR input, and Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking rises from 40.4 to 40.9).
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: the dataset construction and evaluation pipeline partially rely on LVLMs/LLMs (GPT-5.2 for target generation, value extraction, checklist filtering, query generation; GPT-5-mini as judge), which may introduce model-specific biases; they cite prior checklist-driven-evaluation alignment work and their own 93% human-agreement validation as mitigation but this remains a reliance on LLM-generated ground truth and LLM-as-judge scoring rather than fully independent human labeling of the full dataset. The paper's own appendix (Failure Cases, Appendix G) documents that DVA itself is not fully robust: it fails to prevent 'contextual leakage' where a policy-restricted value is inferable from an unrestricted but logically linked fact (e.g., disclosing a prior repurchase program's $10B amount lets a user infer that an identically-valued new program is also $10B, even though DVA correctly redacted the direct statement). DVA's Table 9 result also shows a faithfulness trade-off versus baselines. Source documents are drawn from only two existing corpora (MMLongBench-Doc and SustainableQA), limiting document-genre diversity to four categories (business, research/introduction, financial, administration/industry).

datasets_used: Doc-PP (this paper's constructed benchmark): 443 explicit + 698 implicit queries, 1141 confidential targets, built from 90 PDFs
MMLongBench-Doc (Ma et al., 2024b) - source corpus for long-form PDFs
SustainableQA (Ali et al., 2025) - source corpus for long-form PDFs
quantitative_results: Explicit-query leakage w/ policy, image input: GPT-5.2 8.1, Gemini-3-Pro-Preview 31.4, Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking 11.5, Gemini-3-Flash-Preview 49.4, Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct 75.6, Mistral-Large-2512 45.8

Implicit-query leakage w/ policy, image input: GPT-5.2 25.7, Gemini-3-Pro-Preview 46.8, Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking 40.4, Gemini-3-Flash-Preview 64.6, Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct 93.5, Mistral-Large-2512 76.8
OCR vs image explicit leakage: GPT-5.2 11.5 (OCR) vs 8.1 (image); Gemini-3-Pro-Preview 28.7 (OCR) vs 31.4 (image); Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking 14.2 (OCR) vs 11.5 (image)

Evidence-type implicit leakage (Gemini-3-Flash-Preview): Text-Single 72.8, Table-Single 75.2, Chart 67.9, Figure 56.7, Text-Multi 84.6, Table-Multi 100.0, Table+Text 88.0, Chart+Text 100.0, Figure+Text 83.3

Document-type leakage (Gemini-3-Flash-Preview): Research report 62.2%, Financial report 77.5%, Business report 84.6%, Administration/Industry file 72.0%; text ratios 50.3%, 60.7%, 68.1%, 86.0% respectively

Mitigation leakage (default -> CoT -> Revision -> DVA): Gemini-3-Flash-Preview 64.6 -> 51.0 -> 38.8 -> 30.5; Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct 93.5 -> 50.6 -> 44.5 -> 24.5; Mistral-Large-2512 76.8 -> 70.6 -> 41.9 -> 41.6
Average response length (tokens): Naive 382.42, CoT 551.67, Revision 862.36, DVA 935.97
Aggregated 3-model leakage/faithfulness: CoT 51.0 leak / 59.0 faithful; Revision 38.8 leak / 66.9 faithful; DVA 30.5 leak / 55.8 faithful
Checklist filtering: 838 candidate targets reduced to 635 retained after checklist verification
Human validation agreement: 93% agreement between LLM-as-judge and human annotators (100 sampled instances, both generation-stage and evaluation-stage studies)
baselines_compared: Post-hoc output Revision (adapted from Chang et al., 2025 methodology)
DVA (Decompose-Verify-Aggregation) - the paper's own proposed method, compared against the above three
